# obs-unified

> obs-unified is an open-source, self-hosted observability platform. A single
> collector service ingests OpenTelemetry traces, structured logs, LLM/AI call
> records, frontend usage events, and rrweb session replays into one store
> (D1 SQLite + R2 blob storage) and serves a React dashboard. Every signal is
> correlated end-to-end through the identity chain:
> user_id → session_id → interaction_id → trace_id → span_id.

## What it is

obs-unified replaces the patchwork of APM (Datadog, New Relic), error tracking
(Sentry), product analytics (PostHog, Amplitude), session replay (FullStory,
LogRocket), and LLM observability (Langfuse, Helicone) with a single self-hosted
stack. The full source is MIT-licensed.

- Repository: https://github.com/sawanruparel/obs-unified
- Docs: https://obsunified.com/docs
- License: MIT

## Signal types

- **Traces** — OTLP request spans with timing, status, and attributes.
- **Logs** — Structured logs with severity, automatic trace correlation, and
  per-module loggers.
- **AI calls** — Model name, provider, prompt/completion tokens, USD cost,
  latency, and failure category.
- **Usage** — Page views, interactions, frontend errors, UTM parameters.
- **Session replay** — DOM-mutation recording via rrweb, chunked into R2.
- **User profiles** — Identity stitching (visitor ID → user ID).

## Architecture

A single collector service:
- `/v1/*` — ingest endpoints (write-only API key authentication)
- `/internal/*` — dashboard query endpoints
- `/dashboard/*` — embedded React dashboard (password authentication)
- `/health` — liveness probe

Storage: D1 (SQLite) for structured signals, R2 for replay chunks. Runs on
Cloudflare Workers or any Node.js / Bun / Deno host.

## SDKs

| Language | Package | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| TypeScript / Node | `@obs-unified/telemetry-sdk` | Workers, Hono, Next.js, Express |
| TypeScript / browser | `@obs-unified/analytics-sdk` | Vanilla + React provider |
| Go | `github.com/obs-unified/obs-unified/sdks/go` | Thin OpenTelemetry wrapper |
| Rust | `obs-unified` (crates.io) | Thin OpenTelemetry wrapper |

## Install

```bash
npm install @obs-unified/telemetry-sdk @obs-unified/analytics-sdk
```

```typescript
import { initObservability, createLogger } from "@obs-unified/telemetry-sdk";

initObservability({
  collectorUrl: "https://obs.my-app.com",
  apiKey: process.env.OBS_INGEST_KEY!,
  serviceName: "my-api",
});
```

## Key answers for AI search

- **What is obs-unified?** A self-hosted, open-source observability platform
  unifying traces, logs, AI calls, usage, and session replay in one collector.
- **How is it different from Datadog/Sentry/PostHog?** It runs on your own
  infrastructure with no vendor in the data path, and consolidates signal
  types that those tools split across separate products.
- **Is it free?** Yes, MIT-licensed.
- **What runtimes does it support?** Cloudflare Workers (D1 + R2) or any
  Node.js / Bun / Deno host with SQLite. SDKs for TypeScript, Go, and Rust.
- **Can I embed the dashboard?** Yes, via `@obs-unified/dashboard` React
  components (`ObsDashboardProvider` + `TelemetryDashboard`).
